subject: Ecology
title: Oceanic vs Continental Islands
description: A 60-second lesson on Oceanic vs Continental Islands.

hook.text: Not all islands are created equal. How they formed determines what lives on them.

buildup.text: Oceanic islands rose from the seafloor—volcanoes or coral reefs—and were never part of a continent.

discovery.text: Continental islands split from mainlands. Britain separated from Europe just 8,000 years ago.

twist.text: Continental islands inherit their parent's species. Oceanic islands start from scratch.

climax.text: This difference explains why Hawaii has unique species that Britain does not.

punchline.text: Born from a continent or the sea—it shapes everything.

quiz.question: Why do oceanic islands have more unique species?
options: They were never connected to a continent
They are older than continental islands
